title: eriophorum × medium var. rufescens
intro: Eriophorum × medium var. rufescens, also known as the red-stemmed cotton grass, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Eriophorum, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family. Traditionally, the roots and leaves of Eriophorum × medium var. rufescens have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried powder or in teas, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to make salves and poultices.
